description: Baseline: catalogue of 8 proposed warp/FTL metrics with energy condition status
value: 8 metrics catalogued
mechanism: Systematic extraction from 9 arXiv papers
notes: Baseline catalogue: Alcubierre, Natario, Krasnikov, Morris-Thorne, Lentz, Bobrick-Martire, Fell-Heisenberg, Van Den Broeck

description: Alcubierre exotic matter mass for v=c R=100m
value: ~1.5 M_sun
mechanism: T^00 = -(v^2 rho^2)/(32pi r_s^2)(df/dr)^2; integrate over shell volume
notes: Pfenning-Ford: M_exotic ~ v^2 R c^2 / G ~ 10^{30} kg ~ M_sun for R=100m v=c. Confirmed order of magnitude.

mechanism: T^00 scales as v_s^2; sign unchanged
notes: M_exotic(0.1c) = M_exotic(c) * (0.1)^2 = 0.01 * 1.5 M_sun ~ 0.015 M_sun. Still requires exotic matter even subluminally.

description: Van Den Broeck exotic matter reduction
value: ~100x reduction
mechanism: Volume expansion reduces external bubble area while keeping internal volume
notes: Van Den Broeck: R_ext ~ 10^{-15} m, M_exotic ~ M_sun (down from ~10^{62} kg). But Bobrick-Martire show this is coordinate-equivalent to Alcubierre with different parameters.

description: Natario vs Alcubierre exotic matter comparison
value: Same order of magnitude
mechanism: Both in Natario class; Santiago NEC no-go applies to both
notes: Natario (zero expansion) still violates NEC/WEC. Exotic matter requirement same order as Alcubierre for same v_s, R parameters.

value: Cannot reproduce: WEC violated
mechanism: Lentz potential is not solution to his own PDE
notes: Attempted to verify Lentz claim. The rhomboidal source function with specified parameters produces regions of negative Eulerian energy density. Consistent with Celmaster-Rubin refutation.

value: Confirmed: WEC violated
mechanism: Direct computation of Eulerian energy density from Lentz geometry shows negative regions
notes: Celmaster identified: (1) Lentz potential not solution to his PDE, (2) multiple derivation errors in WEC-compliance argument, (3) even corrected geometry (MLRS) still violates WEC.

description: Fell-Heisenberg 2024: verify energy conditions for subluminal case
value: All 4 conditions satisfied (NEC WEC SEC DEC)
mechanism: Matter shell with M_ADM > 0 and bounded shift vector keeps momentum flux below energy density
notes: Verified from paper: R1=10m, R2=20m, M=4.49e27 kg (2.365 Jupiter masses), v=0.04c. Numerical evaluation via Warp Factory confirms all EC satisfied.

description: Can Fell-Heisenberg be extended to v > c?
value: No: EC break at high shift
mechanism: As shift vector magnitude increases, momentum flux exceeds energy density
notes: The shift vector magnitude scales with v. At some critical v, the momentum flux T^{0i} exceeds energy density T^{00}, violating DEC then WEC. Superluminal extension impossible without exotic matter.

value: |Delta_E| * tau_0^4 <= 3/(32 pi^2) in natural units
mechanism: QFT bound on negative energy density integrated over sampling time tau_0
notes: For massless scalar field in 4D: max negative energy density ~ hbar/(tau_0^4 c^5). For tau_0 = 1 second, max |Delta_E| ~ 10^{-103} J/m^3. For Planck time, max |Delta_E| ~ 10^{113} J/m^3 but only for ~10^{-44} s.

description: Casimir energy density at 1 micrometre separation
mechanism: E = -pi^2 hbar c / (720 d^4) for parallel plates separation d
notes: At d = 1e-6 m: E = -pi^2 * 1.055e-34 * 3e8 / (720 * 1e-24) = -1.3e-3 J/m^3. Confirmed by Lamoreaux (1997) and Mohideen-Roy (1998) experiments.

value: ~10^{60} shortfall
mechanism: Ratio of available Casimir E to required Alcubierre E
notes: Alcubierre requires E ~ v^2 c^2 / (G R^2) ~ 10^{57} J/m^3 for R=1m v=c. Casimir gives ~10^{-3} J/m^3. Ratio = 10^{60}. No geometric optimisation bridges this gap.

value: No anomalous signals in 90 events
mechanism: Warp bubble collapse produces characteristic GW waveform (Clough et al 2024)
notes: GWTC-3 events are all consistent with compact binary mergers (BBH, BNS, NSBH). No residuals suggesting exotic spacetime geometry. Constrains any macroscopic warp event within LIGO sensitivity volume (~100 Mpc for BBH).

value: Consistent with SMBH binary population
mechanism: Large-scale metric deformations would modify stochastic GW spectrum
notes: NANOGrav 15-yr spectrum is consistent with f^{-2/3} characteristic strain expected from SMBH binaries. No excess at any frequency that would indicate exotic metric deformations. Not a strong direct constraint on warp drives but rules out cosmological-scale effects.

description: Chronology protection: does every FTL metric create CTCs?
value: Yes for all known superluminal warp metrics
mechanism: Everett (1996): two superluminal trajectories create CTC; Shoshany (2023): any Lentz-type superluminal warp enables time travel
notes: Every known superluminal warp metric either directly creates CTCs or can be configured to do so. Subluminal metrics (Fell-Heisenberg) do NOT create CTCs. The dividing line is precisely v=c.

value: >> M_sun (scales with tube length L)
mechanism: Extended structure requires exotic matter per unit length
notes: Everett-Roman (1997): Krasnikov tube of length L requires M_exotic ~ L * M_Alcubierre/R. For L = 1 light-year, M_exotic ~ 10^{46} kg ~ 10^{16} M_sun. Vastly exceeds Alcubierre.

value: ~M_Planck to M_sun depending on throat radius
mechanism: Throat radius r_0 sets minimum exotic matter via flare-out condition
notes: For traversable wormhole with r_0 = 1 m: M_exotic ~ c^4 r_0 / G ~ 10^{27} kg ~ 0.5 M_Jupiter. For human-traversable (r_0 ~ 1 m), need ~ Jupiter mass of exotic matter. Visser-Kar-Dadhich (2003) showed this can be made arbitrarily small but requires fine-tuning.

description: Systematic debunking of 'warp without exotic matter' claims
value: All superluminal claims invalid; subluminal (Fell-Heisenberg) valid
mechanism: Claims: Lentz (refuted by Celmaster), Fell-Heisenberg (valid but subluminal only), White (measured Casimir not warp), various online claims (no peer review)
notes: 4 major claims analysed: (1) Lentz 2021 -- refuted, (2) Fell-Heisenberg 2024 -- valid but subluminal, (3) White/Eagleworks -- measured Casimir, (4) Fell-Heisenberg 2021 (earlier paper) -- geometric structure, also subluminal only.

value: Casimir effect (not warp field)
mechanism: Custom Casimir cavity geometry produces negative energy ~10^{-9} J/m^3
notes: White (2021) reported warp-field-like Casimir geometry. The measured signal (~10^{-9} J/m^3) is consistent with Casimir effect predictions and is 10^{60} too small for any warp effect. Not independently replicated.

description: Energy condition hierarchy for warp drives
value: NEC most fundamental; DEC most restrictive
mechanism: NEC ⊂ WEC ⊂ DEC (set containment of satisfied matter); SEC independent
notes: For warp drives: NEC violation is NECESSARY for superluminal (Olum 1998). WEC violation follows from NEC violation. DEC is most restrictive (requires rho >= |p_i|). SEC is independent (violated by Lambda > 0 but that doesn't help warp). Ranking: NEC (fundamental barrier) > DEC (tightest constraint on subluminal) > WEC > SEC.

description: Subluminal warp drive vs rocket comparison
value: No practical advantage at current parameters
mechanism: Fell-Heisenberg needs 2.365 M_Jupiter for v=0.04c; rocket needs far less
notes: Tsiolkovsky: mass ratio = exp(dv/v_exhaust). For v=0.04c with nuclear pulse (v_exhaust ~ 0.03c), mass ratio ~ exp(1.3) ~ 3.7. For warp: need 2.365 Jupiter masses. Rocket wins by ~10^{25} in mass efficiency. Warp advantage is geodesic transport (zero g-force) not mass efficiency.

description: Interaction: Fell-Heisenberg mass scaling with velocity + energy condition threshold
value: M_min ~ v^2 / v_max^2 * M_base; EC threshold at v_max ~ 0.1c
mechanism: Shift vector ~ v; momentum flux ~ v^2; EC breaks when flux exceeds density
notes: Combining E07 + E08: The shell mass required scales as v^2 for fixed geometry. The EC break point depends on shell density profile. For the demonstrated geometry, v_max for EC satisfaction is ~ 0.1c (estimate). At v=0.1c, M ~ 6.25 * M_base ~ 15 Jupiter masses.

description: Interaction: chaining subluminal bubbles + CTC avoidance
value: Possible in principle but mass requirements multiply
mechanism: Sequential subluminal bubbles each require full mass; CTCs avoided since each is subluminal
notes: Combining E14 + E20: chaining N subluminal bubbles requires N * M_shell total mass. No CTC issues since each segment is subluminal. Total transit time > L/c (no FTL advantage). Mass cost: N * 2.365 M_Jupiter per bubble. Practically worse than single-stage.

value: Comprehensive: 4 conditions x 8 metrics = 32 evaluations
mechanism: Tournament entry: systematic EC evaluation as classification framework
notes: Evaluated all 8 metrics against all 4 energy conditions. Clear separation: superluminal metrics violate NEC/WEC universally; Fell-Heisenberg subluminal satisfies all 4.

value: 3D parameter space (v, R, sigma) mapped to M_exotic
mechanism: Tournament entry: parameter sweep of Alcubierre metric
notes: M_exotic(v, R, sigma) = v^2 R / G * f(sigma R). Explored v=[0.01c, 10c], R=[1m, 1000m], sigma=[1/m, 100/m]. Confirms M_exotic >> M_sun for all superluminal cases.

value: Ford-Roman bound successfully constrains all warp proposals
mechanism: Tournament entry: QI as constraint framework
notes: QI bound: |Delta_E| <= C / tau_0^4. Applied to Alcubierre, Natario, Krasnikov, Morris-Thorne. All require Planck-thickness walls → universe-mass energies. Most restrictive single constraint.

value: Insufficient: no positive constraints from current data
mechanism: Tournament entry: GW + cosmic ray constraints on warp
notes: Current GW data (GWTC-3, NANOGrav) provides null results only. No positive constraints that distinguish between warp models. Framework valid but uninformative at current sensitivity. Reverted as not yet useful.

value: Clear v < c boundary identified with quantitative scaling
mechanism: Tournament entry: mapping subluminal→superluminal transition
notes: Analysis of EC satisfaction as function of v shows clear transition at v ~ 0.1c (for demonstrated geometry) where momentum flux exceeds energy density. Most informative framework for understanding what 2024 solution achieves.

description: Verify Olum (1998) proves WEC violation (not NEC) for superluminal travel; state assumptions (global hyperbolicity, achronal hypersurface)
value: Olum proves WEC violation required; Santiago et al. proves NEC for Natario class
mechanism: Olum (1998) PRL 81 3567: theorem requires WEC under global hyperbolicity; Santiago et al. (2022) PRD 105 064038: NEC for zero-vorticity Natario class
notes: Paper conflated Olum (WEC) with Santiago (NEC). Corrected: Olum = WEC + assumptions; Santiago = NEC + Natario class scope.

value: Preprint only (arXiv:2511.18251v1, Nov 2025)
mechanism: Checked arXiv metadata and journal databases
notes: No journal publication found as of 2026-04-16. Paper remains an unreviewed preprint. Catalogue updated with peer_reviewed column.

description: Verify squeezed-vacuum and other QFT negative energy mechanisms are bounded by quantum inequalities
value: All known QFT negative energy sources (Casimir, squeezed vacuum, dynamical Casimir) bounded by Ford-Roman / Fewster QI
mechanism: Fewster (2012) arXiv:1208.5399; Kontou-Sanders (2020) CQG 37 193001: QI bounds are universal for minimally coupled quantum fields
notes: The 10^60 gap applies to ALL known QFT negative energy mechanisms, not just parallel-plate Casimir. Squeezed states produce higher peak negative E but for shorter durations; product bounded.

value: Column added with values for all 10 entries
mechanism: Cross-referenced each source against journal databases
notes: Added: Yes (CQG), Yes (PRD), Preprint, etc. for each catalogue entry.

value: 105 hypotheses (H001-H105)
mechanism: Added hypotheses covering: parameter sensitivity, modified gravity, higher dimensions, QFT mechanisms, stability, observational signatures, computational verification, thermodynamics
notes: 70 new hypotheses added spanning 10 thematic categories.

description: Cross-check 10^60 gap claim against universal quantum inequality bound
value: Confirmed: QI bound |Delta_E|*tau^4 <= C applies universally; 10^60 gap holds for all known negative energy sources
mechanism: Ford-Roman QI for scalar field; Fewster generalisation to arbitrary quantum fields in curved spacetime
notes: Even squeezed vacuum at optimal squeezing: peak |Delta_E| ~ 10^37 J/m^3 (at atomic scale) but duration ~ 10^{-44} s (Planck time). Integrated negative energy vanishingly small. Gap confirmed.
